---
title: "FastAPI"
subtitle: "Modern, Fast, Async Python Web APIs"
author: "Python with FastAPI"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [dark, custom.scss]
    highlight-style: monokai
    filters:
      - code-fullscreen
    transition: slide
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: false
    footer: "FastAPI — Complete Guide"
    progress: true
    code-fold: false
    code-line-numbers: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    incremental: false
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.05
    center: false
  pptx:
    mermaid-format: png
mermaid:
  theme: dark

execute:
  echo: false
  eval: true
---

## What is FastAPI? {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**FastAPI** is a modern, high-performance Python web framework for building **APIs** with Python type hints.

<br>

::: {.fragment}
**Core Philosophy**

- **Fast to run** — one of the fastest Python frameworks (NodeJS / Go level)
- **Fast to code** — type hints + auto-completion reduce bugs by ~40%
- **Standards-based** — OpenAPI, JSON Schema, OAuth2 out of the box
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Created in 2018** by Sebastián Ramírez  
Built on **Starlette** (ASGI) and **Pydantic** (data validation)  
Fully **async/await** native
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Used by:** Microsoft, Uber, Netflix, Apple, NASA, Explosion AI
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
mindmap
  root((FastAPI))
    Performance
      Async/Await
      ASGI
      Starlette
    Validation
      Pydantic v2
      Type Hints
      JSON Schema
    API Docs
      Swagger UI
      ReDoc
      OpenAPI 3
    Features
      Dependency Injection
      OAuth2/JWT
      WebSockets
      Background Tasks
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## FastAPI vs Other Frameworks {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

| Feature | FastAPI | Django REST | Flask | Express (Node) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Type | Async API | Full-stack | Micro | Micro |
| Performance | ⚡ Excellent | Good | Good | Excellent |
| Async Native | ✅ Yes | Partial | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |
| Auto API Docs | ✅ Built-in | ❌ Manual | ❌ Manual | ❌ Manual |
| Data Validation | ✅ Pydantic | DRF Serializers | 3rd-party | 3rd-party |
| Type Safety | ✅ Full | Partial | ❌ None | Partial (TS) |
| Learning Curve | Low | Medium | Low | Low |
| ORM | 3rd-party | Built-in | 3rd-party | 3rd-party |

::: {.fragment}
> **Choose FastAPI** when building APIs, microservices, or any service where performance, type safety, and auto-generated documentation matter.
:::

---

## Architecture Overview {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark', 'flowchart': {'curve': 'orthogonal'}}}%%
graph TD
    CLIENT["🌐 Client"] -->|HTTP Request| ASGI
    ASGI["⚡ ASGI Server<br/>(Uvicorn / Hypercorn)"] --> MW
    MW["🛡️ Middleware Stack"] --> ROUTER
    ROUTER["🔀 APIRouter<br/>(path matching)"] --> DEP
    DEP["💉 Dependency<br/>Injection"] --> VIEW
    VIEW["⚙️ Path Operation<br/>Function"] --> PYDANTIC
    PYDANTIC["✅ Pydantic<br/>(request validation)"] --> VIEW
    VIEW --> DB["🗄️ Database/<br/>External Services"]
    DB --> VIEW
    VIEW --> RESP["📦 Response<br/>(Pydantic serialization)"]
    RESP --> CLIENT

    classDef client  fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef asgi    fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef core    fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    classDef data    fill:#16213e,stroke:#aaa,color:#ccc
    class CLIENT client
    class ASGI,MW asgi
    class ROUTER,DEP,VIEW,PYDANTIC core
    class DB,RESP data
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.fragment}
**FastAPI's two foundation libraries:**

**Starlette** — the ASGI web toolkit underneath:

- Request/Response objects
- Routing
- Middleware
- WebSockets
- Background tasks
- Static files
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Pydantic v2** — the data layer:

- Data validation via type hints
- JSON Schema generation
- Settings management
- Auto-serialization of responses
:::

::: {.fragment}
> FastAPI = Starlette + Pydantic + automatic OpenAPI generation
:::
:::
:::

---

## Request–Response Lifecycle {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark', 'sequence': {'mirrorActors': false}}}%%
sequenceDiagram
    participant C   as 🌐 Client
    participant UV  as ⚡ Uvicorn (ASGI)
    participant MW  as 🛡️ Middleware
    participant R   as 🔀 Router
    participant DI  as 💉 Dependency Injection
    participant PV  as ⚙️ Path Operation
    participant PD  as ✅ Pydantic
    participant DB  as 🗄️ Database

    C  ->> UV : HTTP Request
    UV ->> MW : ASGI scope + receive
    MW ->> R  : process_request()
    R  ->> DI : route match → resolve dependencies
    DI ->> PV : inject resolved deps into function
    PV ->> PD : parse + validate path/query/body params
    PD -->> PV: validated Python objects
    PV ->> DB : async query (await db.execute(...))
    DB -->> PV: result rows
    PV -->> PD: serialize response model
    PD -->> MW: validated JSON response
    MW -->> UV: process_response()
    UV -->> C : HTTP Response (JSON)
```

---

## Project Structure {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Install & create**
```bash
pip install fastapi uvicorn[standard]
pip install sqlalchemy alembic pydantic-settings
```

::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph LR
    ROOT["📁 myapp/"] --> MAIN["main.py"]
    ROOT --> CORE["📁 core/"]
    ROOT --> API["📁 api/"]
    ROOT --> MODELS["📁 models/"]
    ROOT --> SCHEMAS["📁 schemas/"]
    ROOT --> CRUD["📁 crud/"]
    ROOT --> DB["📁 db/"]
    ROOT --> DEPS["dependencies.py"]
    ROOT --> CONFIG["config.py"]
    ROOT --> TESTS["📁 tests/"]

    CORE --> SECURITY["security.py"]
    CORE --> SETTINGS["settings.py"]
    API  --> V1["📁 v1/"]
    V1   --> USERS["users.py"]
    V1   --> POSTS["posts.py"]
    V1   --> AUTH["auth.py"]
    DB   --> SESSION["session.py"]
    DB   --> BASE["base.py"]

    classDef file fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    classDef dir  fill:#1a1a2e,stroke:#533483,color:#ccc
    class MAIN,DEPS,CONFIG,SECURITY,SETTINGS,USERS,POSTS,AUTH,SESSION,BASE file
    class ROOT,CORE,API,MODELS,SCHEMAS,CRUD,DB,V1,TESTS dir
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.fragment}
**Key files explained:**

| File / Dir | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `main.py` | App factory, routers, startup |
| `core/settings.py` | Pydantic settings config |
| `core/security.py` | JWT, hashing utilities |
| `api/v1/` | Route handlers per resource |
| `models/` | SQLAlchemy ORM models |
| `schemas/` | Pydantic request/response schemas |
| `crud/` | Reusable DB access functions |
| `db/session.py` | Async DB engine + session |
| `dependencies.py` | Shared FastAPI dependencies |
| `tests/` | pytest test suite |
:::
:::
:::

---

## Your First FastAPI App {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
# main.py
from fastapi import FastAPI
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager

@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    # Startup: initialise DB pool, load ML models, etc.
    print("Starting up...")
    yield
    # Shutdown: close connections, flush cache
    print("Shutting down...")

app = FastAPI(
    title="My Blog API",
    description="A blog API built with FastAPI",
    version="1.0.0",
    lifespan=lifespan,
)

# Include routers
from api.v1 import posts, users, auth
app.include_router(auth.router,  prefix="/api/v1/auth",  tags=["Auth"])
app.include_router(users.router, prefix="/api/v1/users", tags=["Users"])
app.include_router(posts.router, prefix="/api/v1/posts", tags=["Posts"])

@app.get("/health", tags=["Health"])
async def health_check():
    return {"status": "ok"}
```

::: {.fragment}
```bash
# Run the development server
uvicorn main:app --reload --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000

# Auto-generated docs available at:
# http://localhost:8000/docs    ← Swagger UI
# http://localhost:8000/redoc  ← ReDoc
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph LR
    APP["FastAPI app"] --> STARTUP["lifespan startup<br/>(DB pool, ML models)"]
    APP --> ROUTERS["include_router()<br/>/api/v1/auth<br/>/api/v1/users<br/>/api/v1/posts"]
    APP --> DOCS["Auto-generated Docs<br/>/docs → Swagger UI<br/>/redoc → ReDoc<br/>/openapi.json"]
    APP --> SHUTDOWN["lifespan shutdown<br/>(close connections)"]

    classDef root  fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef node  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef doc   fill:#0a3d0a,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    class APP root
    class STARTUP,ROUTERS,SHUTDOWN node
    class DOCS doc
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
> FastAPI runs on **ASGI** — Unlike WSGI (Django, Flask), ASGI supports true async I/O, WebSockets, and HTTP/2.
:::
:::
:::

---

## Path Operations & Routing {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
# api/v1/posts.py
from fastapi import APIRouter, status

router = APIRouter()

# GET  /posts/            → list all posts
@router.get("/", response_model=list[PostOut])
async def list_posts(db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
    return await crud.post.get_multi(db)

# POST /posts/            → create a post
@router.post("/", response_model=PostOut,
             status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
async def create_post(post: PostCreate,
                      db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
                      current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user)):
    return await crud.post.create(db, obj_in=post,
                                   author_id=current_user.id)

# GET  /posts/{post_id}  → get one post
@router.get("/{post_id}", response_model=PostOut)
async def get_post(post_id: int,
                   db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
    post = await crud.post.get(db, id=post_id)
    if not post:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404,
                            detail="Post not found")
    return post

# PATCH /posts/{post_id} → partial update
@router.patch("/{post_id}", response_model=PostOut)
async def update_post(post_id: int, post: PostUpdate,
                      db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
    return await crud.post.update(db, id=post_id, obj_in=post)

# DELETE /posts/{post_id}
@router.delete("/{post_id}", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
async def delete_post(post_id: int,
                      db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
    await crud.post.remove(db, id=post_id)
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph LR
    ROUTER["APIRouter<br/>/posts"] --> GET_L["GET /<br/>list_posts()"]
    ROUTER --> POST["POST /<br/>create_post()"]
    ROUTER --> GET_D["GET /{post_id}<br/>get_post()"]
    ROUTER --> PATCH["PATCH /{post_id}<br/>update_post()"]
    ROUTER --> DELETE["DELETE /{post_id}<br/>delete_post()"]

    classDef router fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef get    fill:#0a3d0a,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    classDef post_  fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef update fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef del_   fill:#3d0a0a,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    class ROUTER router
    class GET_L,GET_D get
    class POST post_
    class PATCH update
    class DELETE del_
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**HTTP method decorators:**  
`@router.get()` — read data  
`@router.post()` — create data  
`@router.put()` — full replace  
`@router.patch()` — partial update  
`@router.delete()` — remove  
`@router.options()` — capability check  
:::
:::
:::

---

## Path, Query & Header Parameters {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
from fastapi import APIRouter, Query, Path, Header
from typing import Annotated

router = APIRouter()

# PATH PARAMETER — part of the URL
# GET /posts/42
@router.get("/{post_id}")
async def get_post(
    post_id: Annotated[int, Path(title="Post ID", ge=1)],
):
    ...

# QUERY PARAMETERS — after the ?
# GET /posts?skip=0&limit=10&status=published
@router.get("/")
async def list_posts(
    skip:   Annotated[int, Query(ge=0, default=0)],
    limit:  Annotated[int, Query(ge=1, le=100, default=10)],
    status: Annotated[str | None, Query()] = None,
    q:      Annotated[str | None, Query(min_length=3)] = None,
):
    ...

# HEADER PARAMETERS
@router.get("/protected")
async def protected_route(
    x_token: Annotated[str, Header()],
    user_agent: Annotated[str | None, Header()] = None,
):
    if x_token != "secret-token":
        raise HTTPException(status_code=403)
    ...
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    URL["GET /posts/42?skip=0&limit=10<br/>Headers: X-Token: abc"] --> PATH["Path Params<br/>{post_id} = 42<br/>→ int, validated ≥ 1"]
    URL --> QUERY["Query Params<br/>skip=0, limit=10<br/>→ validated, defaults applied"]
    URL --> HEADER["Header Params<br/>X-Token: abc<br/>User-Agent: ..."]
    PATH  --> FUNC["Path operation<br/>function receives<br/>typed Python values"]
    QUERY --> FUNC
    HEADER --> FUNC

    classDef url    fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef param  fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef func   fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    class URL url
    class PATH,QUERY,HEADER param
    class FUNC func
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Validation constraints:**  
`ge=1` — greater than or equal  
`gt=0` — strictly greater  
`le=100` — less than or equal  
`lt=` — strictly less  
`min_length=` — string min length  
`max_length=` — string max length  
`pattern=` — regex validation  
:::
:::
:::

---

## Pydantic Schemas {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
# schemas/post.py
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum

class PostStatus(str, Enum):
    draft     = "draft"
    published = "published"
    archived  = "archived"

# Base — shared fields
class PostBase(BaseModel):
    title:   str   = Field(..., min_length=5, max_length=200,
                           description="Post title")
    content: str   = Field(..., min_length=10)
    status:  PostStatus = PostStatus.draft

    @field_validator('title')
    @classmethod
    def title_must_not_be_empty(cls, v: str) -> str:
        if v.strip() == '':
            raise ValueError('Title cannot be blank')
        return v.strip()

# Create — what the client sends
class PostCreate(PostBase):
    category_id: int

# Update — all fields optional for PATCH
class PostUpdate(BaseModel):
    title:    str | None       = None
    content:  str | None       = None
    status:   PostStatus | None = None

# Out — what the API returns
class PostOut(PostBase):
    id:         int
    author_id:  int
    created_at: datetime
    updated_at: datetime

    model_config = {"from_attributes": True}  # ORM mode
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    BASE["PostBase<br/>title, content, status<br/>(shared validation)"]
    BASE --> CREATE["PostCreate<br/>+ category_id<br/>(client → server)"]
    BASE --> UPDATE["PostUpdate<br/>all fields Optional<br/>(PATCH body)"]
    BASE --> OUT["PostOut<br/>+ id, author_id<br/>created_at, updated_at<br/>(server → client)"]

    CREATE -->|"request body"| ENDPOINT["Path Operation<br/>Function"]
    UPDATE -->|"request body"| ENDPOINT
    ENDPOINT -->|"response"| OUT

    classDef base   fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef schema fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef ep     fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    class BASE base
    class CREATE,UPDATE,OUT schema
    class ENDPOINT ep
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Pydantic v2 features used:**  
`Field(...)` — required field (Ellipsis)  
`Field(default=)` — optional with default  
`@field_validator` — custom field logic  
`model_config` — ORM mode, aliases  
`model_json_schema()` — JSON Schema export  
:::
:::
:::

---

## Pydantic Validation Pipeline {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
from pydantic import BaseModel, EmailStr, model_validator
from pydantic import field_validator, Field
from typing import Self

class UserCreate(BaseModel):
    username:         str      = Field(min_length=3, max_length=50)
    email:            EmailStr
    password:         str      = Field(min_length=8)
    password_confirm: str

    @field_validator('username')
    @classmethod
    def username_alphanumeric(cls, v: str) -> str:
        if not v.isalnum():
            raise ValueError('Username must be alphanumeric')
        return v.lower()

    @field_validator('password')
    @classmethod
    def password_strength(cls, v: str) -> str:
        if not any(c.isupper() for c in v):
            raise ValueError('Password needs an uppercase letter')
        if not any(c.isdigit() for c in v):
            raise ValueError('Password needs a digit')
        return v

    @model_validator(mode='after')
    def passwords_match(self) -> Self:
        if self.password != self.password_confirm:
            raise ValueError('Passwords do not match')
        return self

# FastAPI auto-returns 422 Unprocessable Entity on failure:
# {
#   "detail": [
#     {"loc": ["body", "password"], "msg": "...", "type": "value_error"}
#   ]
# }
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
flowchart TD
    JSON["Raw JSON body<br/>from request"] --> PARSE["JSON → dict<br/>(type coercion)"]
    PARSE --> FIELD_V["@field_validator<br/>per-field custom logic"]
    FIELD_V --> MODEL_V["@model_validator<br/>cross-field logic"]
    MODEL_V -->|"pass"| OBJ["Validated Python<br/>object (UserCreate)"]
    MODEL_V -->|"fail"| ERR["422 Unprocessable Entity<br/>{detail: [{loc, msg, type}]}"]
    OBJ --> FUNC["Path operation<br/>receives typed object"]

    classDef input  fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef step   fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef ok     fill:#0a3d0a,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    classDef fail   fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    class JSON,PARSE input
    class FIELD_V,MODEL_V step
    class OBJ,FUNC ok
    class ERR fail
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
> FastAPI **automatically** returns 422 with detailed error info when Pydantic validation fails — no error handling code required.
:::
:::
:::

---

## Response Models & Status Codes {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
from fastapi import APIRouter, status
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse, Response

router = APIRouter()

# response_model filters & validates the output
@router.get("/{user_id}", response_model=UserOut)
async def get_user(user_id: int, db=Depends(get_db)):
    user = await crud.user.get(db, id=user_id)
    if not user:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404,
                            detail="User not found")
    return user  # SQLAlchemy ORM object → Pydantic serializes it

# Custom status code
@router.post("/", response_model=UserOut,
             status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED)
async def create_user(user: UserCreate, db=Depends(get_db)):
    return await crud.user.create(db, obj_in=user)

# Exclude fields from response
@router.get("/me", response_model=UserOut,
            response_model_exclude={"hashed_password", "is_superuser"})
async def get_me(current_user=Depends(get_current_user)):
    return current_user

# 204 No Content — no response body
@router.delete("/{user_id}",
               status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)
async def delete_user(user_id: int, db=Depends(get_db)):
    await crud.user.remove(db, id=user_id)
    return Response(status_code=204)
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    RETURN["return orm_object / dict"] --> RMODEL["response_model=UserOut<br/>(Pydantic)"]
    RMODEL --> FILTER["Filter out<br/>excluded fields<br/>hashed_password, etc."]
    FILTER --> VALIDATE["Validate /<br/>serialize to JSON"]
    VALIDATE --> STATUS["Apply status_code<br/>200 / 201 / 204"]
    STATUS --> RESP["JSON HTTP Response<br/>Content-Type: application/json"]

    classDef step  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef resp  fill:#0a3d0a,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    classDef start fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    class RETURN start
    class RMODEL,FILTER,VALIDATE,STATUS step
    class RESP resp
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Common status codes:**  
`200 OK` — default, successful read  
`201 Created` — successful creation  
`204 No Content` — successful delete  
`400 Bad Request` — client error  
`401 Unauthorized` — not authenticated  
`403 Forbidden` — not authorized  
`404 Not Found` — resource missing  
`422 Unprocessable Entity` — validation fail  
:::
:::
:::

---

## Dependency Injection {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
# dependencies.py
from fastapi import Depends, HTTPException, status
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from db.session import async_session_maker
from core.security import decode_access_token
from models.user import User
import crud

# ─── Database session dependency ───────────────────────────
async def get_db() -> AsyncSession:
    async with async_session_maker() as session:
        try:
            yield session
            await session.commit()
        except Exception:
            await session.rollback()
            raise

# ─── Current user dependency ───────────────────────────────
async def get_current_user(
    token: str = Depends(oauth2_scheme),
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
) -> User:
    payload = decode_access_token(token)
    if payload is None:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="Invalid or expired token",
        )
    user = await crud.user.get(db, id=payload["sub"])
    if not user:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="User not found")
    return user

# ─── Admin-only dependency ─────────────────────────────────
async def require_admin(
    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user)
) -> User:
    if not current_user.is_superuser:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Admin only")
    return current_user

# ─── Usage in path operation ───────────────────────────────
@router.get("/admin/stats")
async def admin_stats(
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
    admin: User = Depends(require_admin),
):
    return await crud.get_stats(db)
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    PO["Path Operation<br/>admin_stats()"] -->|"Depends(get_db)"| DB["get_db()<br/>→ AsyncSession"]
    PO -->|"Depends(require_admin)"| ADMIN["require_admin()"]
    ADMIN -->|"Depends(get_current_user)"| AUTH["get_current_user()"]
    AUTH  -->|"Depends(oauth2_scheme)"| TOKEN["OAuth2PasswordBearer<br/>→ JWT token string"]
    AUTH  -->|"Depends(get_db)"| DB

    classDef po    fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef dep   fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef leaf  fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    class PO po
    class ADMIN,AUTH dep
    class DB,TOKEN leaf
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**DI benefits in FastAPI:**
- Automatic dependency resolution & caching
- Dependencies can depend on other dependencies
- Easy to swap for testing (`app.dependency_overrides`)
- Shared resources (DB session, auth) defined once
:::
:::
:::

---

## Authentication — OAuth2 & JWT {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
# core/security.py
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from jose import JWTError, jwt
from passlib.context import CryptContext
from fastapi.security import OAuth2PasswordBearer

SECRET_KEY = "your-secret-key"   # from env in production
ALGORITHM  = "HS256"
ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES = 30

pwd_context    = CryptContext(schemes=["bcrypt"], deprecated="auto")
oauth2_scheme  = OAuth2PasswordBearer(tokenUrl="/api/v1/auth/token")

def hash_password(password: str) -> str:
    return pwd_context.hash(password)

def verify_password(plain: str, hashed: str) -> bool:
    return pwd_context.verify(plain, hashed)

def create_access_token(data: dict) -> str:
    to_encode = data.copy()
    expire = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + \
             timedelta(minutes=ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES)
    to_encode.update({"exp": expire})
    return jwt.encode(to_encode, SECRET_KEY, algorithm=ALGORITHM)

def decode_access_token(token: str) -> dict | None:
    try:
        return jwt.decode(token, SECRET_KEY, algorithms=[ALGORITHM])
    except JWTError:
        return None

# api/v1/auth.py
@router.post("/token", response_model=Token)
async def login(form: OAuth2PasswordRequestForm = Depends(),
                db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
    user = await crud.user.get_by_username(db, form.username)
    if not user or not verify_password(form.password,
                                        user.hashed_password):
        raise HTTPException(status_code=401,
                            detail="Incorrect credentials")
    token = create_access_token({"sub": str(user.id)})
    return {"access_token": token, "token_type": "bearer"}
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark', 'sequence': {'mirrorActors': false}}}%%
sequenceDiagram
    participant C  as Client
    participant A  as /auth/token
    participant R  as Protected Route
    participant DI as get_current_user

    C  ->> A  : POST username + password
    A  ->> A  : verify_password(plain, hashed)
    A  -->> C : {access_token, token_type}
    C  ->> R  : GET /posts<br/>Authorization: Bearer <jwt>
    R  ->> DI : Depends(get_current_user)
    DI ->> DI : decode_access_token(jwt)
    DI ->> DI : load user from DB
    DI -->> R : User object
    R  -->> C : 200 JSON response
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**JWT structure:**  
`Header.Payload.Signature`  
Payload carries: `sub` (user id), `exp` (expiry)  
Never store sensitive data in the payload — it is **base64-encoded, not encrypted**
:::
:::
:::

---

## Database with SQLAlchemy (Async) {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
# db/session.py
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import (
    create_async_engine, async_sessionmaker, AsyncSession
)
DATABASE_URL = "postgresql+asyncpg://user:pass@localhost/mydb"

engine = create_async_engine(DATABASE_URL, echo=False, pool_size=10)
async_session_maker = async_sessionmaker(engine, expire_on_commit=False)

# models/post.py
from sqlalchemy import String, Text, ForeignKey, Enum as SAEnum
from sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column, relationship
from db.base import Base
import datetime

class Post(Base):
    __tablename__ = "posts"

    id:         Mapped[int]      = mapped_column(primary_key=True)
    title:      Mapped[str]      = mapped_column(String(200))
    content:    Mapped[str]      = mapped_column(Text)
    status:     Mapped[str]      = mapped_column(default="draft")
    author_id:  Mapped[int]      = mapped_column(ForeignKey("users.id"))
    created_at: Mapped[datetime.datetime] = mapped_column(
        default=datetime.datetime.utcnow)

    author: Mapped["User"] = relationship(back_populates="posts")

# crud/post.py
from sqlalchemy import select

async def get(db: AsyncSession, *, id: int) -> Post | None:
    result = await db.execute(select(Post).where(Post.id == id))
    return result.scalar_one_or_none()

async def get_multi(db: AsyncSession, *, skip=0, limit=10):
    result = await db.execute(
        select(Post).offset(skip).limit(limit).order_by(Post.created_at.desc())
    )
    return result.scalars().all()

async def create(db: AsyncSession, *, obj_in: PostCreate, author_id: int):
    db_obj = Post(**obj_in.model_dump(), author_id=author_id)
    db.add(db_obj)
    await db.flush()
    return db_obj
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    APP["FastAPI App"] --> ENGINE["create_async_engine<br/>(asyncpg driver)"]
    ENGINE --> POOL["Connection Pool<br/>pool_size=10"]
    POOL --> SESSION["async_sessionmaker<br/>→ AsyncSession"]
    SESSION -->|"Depends(get_db)"| CRUD["CRUD functions<br/>await db.execute(select(...))"]
    CRUD --> MODELS["SQLAlchemy Models<br/>Mapped[] type hints"]
    MODELS --> DB[(PostgreSQL)]

    classDef app   fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef infra fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef code  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef db    fill:#16213e,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    class APP app
    class ENGINE,POOL infra
    class SESSION,CRUD,MODELS code
    class DB db
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**Async DB drivers:**  
PostgreSQL → `asyncpg`  
MySQL → `aiomysql`  
SQLite → `aiosqlite`  
MongoDB → `motor`  
Redis → `redis-py` (async)
:::
:::
:::

---

## Alembic Migrations {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
```bash
# Install & initialise
pip install alembic
alembic init alembic

# Generate a migration from model changes
alembic revision --autogenerate -m "add posts table"

# Apply migrations
alembic upgrade head

# Roll back one step
alembic downgrade -1

# Show migration history
alembic history --verbose
```

::: {.fragment}
```python
# alembic/env.py — key async setup
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import async_engine_from_config
from db.base import Base
import models  # import all models so Base.metadata is populated

target_metadata = Base.metadata

def run_migrations_online():
    connectable = async_engine_from_config(...)
    async with connectable.connect() as connection:
        await connection.run_sync(
            context.run_migrations
        )
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
flowchart TD
    A["Define SQLAlchemy<br/>Model"] --> B["alembic revision<br/>--autogenerate<br/>→ versions/0001_*.py"]
    B --> C["alembic upgrade head<br/>→ CREATE TABLE / ALTER"]
    C --> D["Modify Model<br/>(add column)"]
    D --> E["alembic revision<br/>--autogenerate<br/>→ versions/0002_*.py"]
    E --> F["alembic upgrade head<br/>→ ALTER TABLE"]

    classDef edit fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef cmd  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef db   fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    class A,D edit
    class B,E cmd
    class C,F db
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph LR
    V1["0001_initial"] --> V2["0002_add_status"]
    V2 --> V3["0003_add_tags"]
    V3 --> HEAD["head"]

    classDef ver  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    classDef head fill:#0a3d0a,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    class V1,V2,V3 ver
    class HEAD head
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Async / Await Deep Dive {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
import asyncio
import httpx
from fastapi import APIRouter

router = APIRouter()

# ─── async path operation ──────────────────────────────────
@router.get("/posts/{post_id}")
async def get_post(post_id: int, db = Depends(get_db)):
    # Non-blocking I/O — other requests served during await
    post = await crud.post.get(db, id=post_id)
    return post

# ─── concurrent I/O with asyncio.gather ───────────────────
@router.get("/dashboard")
async def dashboard(user = Depends(get_current_user),
                    db   = Depends(get_db)):
    # Fire 3 DB queries simultaneously — not sequentially!
    posts, comments, stats = await asyncio.gather(
        crud.post.get_by_user(db, user_id=user.id),
        crud.comment.get_recent(db, user_id=user.id),
        crud.analytics.get_user_stats(db, user_id=user.id),
    )
    return {"posts": posts, "comments": comments, "stats": stats}

# ─── calling external APIs asynchronously ─────────────────
@router.get("/weather")
async def get_weather(city: str):
    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
        resp = await client.get(
            f"https://api.weather.com/v1/{city}"
        )
        return resp.json()

# ─── sync functions — use run_in_threadpool automatically ──
@router.get("/sync-example")
def sync_route(data: str):        # No async — runs in thread pool
    result = some_blocking_call(data)
    return result
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    subgraph SYNC["Traditional Sync (WSGI)"]
        R1["Request 1"] --> W1["Wait for DB<br/>(blocking)"]
        W1 --> R1D["Request 1 done"]
        R2["Request 2"] --> W2["Wait for DB<br/>(blocking)"]
    end

    subgraph ASYNC["FastAPI Async (ASGI)"]
        A1["Request 1"] -->|"await db"| LOOP["Event Loop<br/>(free to handle others)"]
        A2["Request 2"] --> LOOP
        A3["Request 3"] --> LOOP
        LOOP -->|"DB responds"| A1D["Request 1 done"]
    end

    classDef sync fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef asy  fill:#0a3d0a,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    class R1,W1,R1D,R2,W2 sync
    class A1,A2,A3,LOOP,A1D asy
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
> Use `async def` for I/O-bound work (DB, HTTP). Use regular `def` for CPU-bound work — FastAPI automatically runs it in a thread pool via `run_in_threadpool`.
:::
:::
:::

---

## Background Tasks {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
from fastapi import APIRouter, BackgroundTasks
import smtplib
from email.message import EmailMessage

router = APIRouter()

# ─── Background task functions ─────────────────────────────
def send_welcome_email(email: str, username: str):
    """Runs after the response is sent — non-blocking for client."""
    msg = EmailMessage()
    msg["Subject"] = f"Welcome, {username}!"
    msg["From"]    = "noreply@myapp.com"
    msg["To"]      = email
    msg.set_content(f"Hi {username}, thanks for signing up!")
    with smtplib.SMTP("localhost") as s:
        s.send_message(msg)

async def update_user_stats(user_id: int, action: str):
    async with async_session_maker() as db:
        await crud.analytics.record_event(db, user_id, action)

# ─── Injecting BackgroundTasks ─────────────────────────────
@router.post("/register", response_model=UserOut,
             status_code=201)
async def register(
    user_in: UserCreate,
    background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
):
    user = await crud.user.create(db, obj_in=user_in)

    # Schedule after response is sent
    background_tasks.add_task(
        send_welcome_email, user.email, user.username
    )
    background_tasks.add_task(
        update_user_stats, user.id, "registered"
    )

    return user   # ← client receives this immediately
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
sequenceDiagram
    participant C as Client
    participant FW as FastAPI
    participant BG as Background Runner

    C  ->> FW : POST /register
    FW ->> FW : create user in DB
    FW ->> BG : schedule send_welcome_email()
    FW ->> BG : schedule update_user_stats()
    FW -->> C : 201 UserOut (instant)
    BG ->> BG : send_welcome_email() (async)
    BG ->> BG : update_user_stats() (async)
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
**When to use BackgroundTasks vs Celery:**

| Scenario | Use |
|---|---|
| Quick fire-and-forget | `BackgroundTasks` |
| Long-running jobs (>5 sec) | Celery |
| Scheduled / periodic tasks | Celery Beat |
| Retries on failure | Celery |
| Distributed workers | Celery |
:::
:::
:::

---

## Middleware {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from fastapi.middleware.gzip import GZipMiddleware
from fastapi.middleware.trustedhost import TrustedHostMiddleware
import time, uuid

app = FastAPI()

# ─── Built-in middleware ────────────────────────────────────
app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=["https://myfrontend.com"],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)
app.add_middleware(GZipMiddleware, minimum_size=1000)
app.add_middleware(TrustedHostMiddleware,
                   allowed_hosts=["myapp.com", "*.myapp.com"])

# ─── Custom middleware ──────────────────────────────────────
@app.middleware("http")
async def add_request_id_and_timing(request: Request, call_next):
    request_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
    request.state.request_id = request_id

    start = time.perf_counter()
    response = await call_next(request)         # run the rest of the stack
    duration = time.perf_counter() - start

    response.headers["X-Request-ID"]  = request_id
    response.headers["X-Process-Time"] = f"{duration:.4f}s"
    return response

# ─── Exception handler (global) ────────────────────────────
from fastapi.exceptions import RequestValidationError
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse

@app.exception_handler(RequestValidationError)
async def validation_exception_handler(request, exc):
    return JSONResponse(
        status_code=422,
        content={"detail": exc.errors(), "body": str(exc.body)},
    )
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    REQ["Incoming Request"] --> TRUSTED["TrustedHostMiddleware<br/>(block unknown hosts)"]
    TRUSTED --> CORS["CORSMiddleware<br/>(set CORS headers)"]
    CORS --> GZIP["GZipMiddleware<br/>(decompress request)"]
    GZIP --> CUSTOM["Custom Middleware<br/>(request ID + timing)"]
    CUSTOM --> APP["Path Operation<br/>Function"]
    APP --> CUSTOM2["Custom Middleware<br/>(inject headers)"]
    CUSTOM2 --> GZIP2["GZipMiddleware<br/>(compress response)"]
    GZIP2 --> CORS2["CORSMiddleware<br/>(set CORS headers)"]
    CORS2 --> RES["Outgoing Response"]

    classDef mw  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef app fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef res fill:#0a3d0a,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    class TRUSTED,CORS,GZIP,CUSTOM,CUSTOM2,GZIP2,CORS2 mw
    class APP app
    class REQ,RES res
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Error Handling {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
from fastapi import HTTPException, status
from fastapi.exceptions import RequestValidationError
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
from pydantic import BaseModel

# ─── Standard HTTPException ────────────────────────────────
@router.get("/posts/{post_id}")
async def get_post(post_id: int, db = Depends(get_db)):
    post = await crud.post.get(db, id=post_id)
    if not post:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
            detail=f"Post {post_id} not found",
            headers={"X-Error": "resource-missing"},
        )
    return post

# ─── Custom exception class ────────────────────────────────
class InsufficientPermissions(Exception):
    def __init__(self, required_role: str):
        self.required_role = required_role

@app.exception_handler(InsufficientPermissions)
async def handle_permission_error(request, exc):
    return JSONResponse(
        status_code=403,
        content={
            "error": "permission_denied",
            "required_role": exc.required_role,
        }
    )

# ─── Override 422 validation error format ─────────────────
@app.exception_handler(RequestValidationError)
async def custom_422(request, exc: RequestValidationError):
    errors = [
        {"field": ".".join(str(l) for l in e["loc"]),
         "message": e["msg"]}
        for e in exc.errors()
    ]
    return JSONResponse(status_code=422,
                        content={"errors": errors})
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
flowchart TD
    REQ["Request"] --> VALID{"Pydantic<br/>Validation?"}
    VALID -->|Fail| V422["422 Unprocessable<br/>RequestValidationError"]
    VALID -->|Pass| VIEW["Path Operation"]
    VIEW --> HTTP{"HTTPException<br/>raised?"}
    HTTP -->|Yes| HTTPRESP["HTTP Error Response<br/>status + detail JSON"]
    HTTP -->|No| CUSTOM{"Custom<br/>Exception?"}
    CUSTOM -->|Yes| HANDLER["Custom exception_handler<br/>→ JSONResponse"]
    CUSTOM -->|No| UNHANDLED["500 Internal<br/>Server Error"]
    VIEW -->|success| OK["200/201 JSON Response"]

    classDef fail fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef ok   fill:#0a3d0a,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    classDef err  fill:#3d0a0a,stroke:#e94560,color:#fff
    classDef step fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    class V422,HTTPRESP,HANDLER,UNHANDLED err
    class OK ok
    class VALID,HTTP,CUSTOM step
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## WebSockets {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
from fastapi import APIRouter, WebSocket, WebSocketDisconnect
from typing import List

router = APIRouter()

# ─── Connection manager for broadcast ─────────────────────
class ConnectionManager:
    def __init__(self):
        self.active: List[WebSocket] = []

    async def connect(self, ws: WebSocket):
        await ws.accept()
        self.active.append(ws)

    def disconnect(self, ws: WebSocket):
        self.active.remove(ws)

    async def broadcast(self, message: str):
        for connection in self.active:
            await connection.send_text(message)

manager = ConnectionManager()

# ─── WebSocket endpoint ────────────────────────────────────
@router.websocket("/ws/chat/{room_id}")
async def chat(websocket: WebSocket, room_id: str,
               token: str):
    # Authenticate via query param token
    user = await get_user_from_token(token)
    if not user:
        await websocket.close(code=1008)
        return

    await manager.connect(websocket)
    try:
        while True:
            data = await websocket.receive_text()
            await manager.broadcast(
                f"[{room_id}] {user.username}: {data}"
            )
    except WebSocketDisconnect:
        manager.disconnect(websocket)
        await manager.broadcast(f"{user.username} left the room")
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark', 'sequence': {'mirrorActors': false}}}%%
sequenceDiagram
    participant C1 as Client 1
    participant C2 as Client 2
    participant S  as FastAPI WS

    C1 ->> S : WS Connect /ws/chat/room1
    S  -->> C1: accept()
    C2 ->> S : WS Connect /ws/chat/room1
    S  -->> C2: accept()
    C1 ->> S : send_text("Hello!")
    S  -->> C1: broadcast "Alice: Hello!"
    S  -->> C2: broadcast "Alice: Hello!"
    C2 ->> S : send_text("Hi there!")
    S  -->> C1: broadcast "Bob: Hi there!"
    S  -->> C2: broadcast "Bob: Hi there!"
    C1 ->> S : disconnect
    S  -->> C2: "Alice left the room"
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## OpenAPI & Automatic Docs {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
from fastapi import FastAPI
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

app = FastAPI(
    title="Blog API",
    description="""
## Blog API

Manage posts, users, and comments.

### Features
- 🔐 JWT Authentication
- 📄 Full CRUD for posts
- 📊 Analytics endpoints
    """,
    version="2.1.0",
    contact={"name": "API Team", "email": "api@myapp.com"},
    license_info={"name": "MIT"},
)

# Enrich docs with Field descriptions
class PostCreate(BaseModel):
    title: str = Field(
        ...,
        min_length=5,
        description="Post title (5–200 chars)",
        examples=["My First FastAPI Post"],
    )
    content: str = Field(
        ..., description="Full post body in Markdown"
    )

# Tag endpoints for grouping in Swagger UI
@router.get(
    "/posts",
    tags=["Posts"],
    summary="List all published posts",
    description="Returns a paginated list of posts. "
                "Supports filtering by status and category.",
    response_description="Paginated list of PostOut objects",
    responses={
        200: {"description": "Successful response"},
        401: {"description": "Not authenticated"},
    },
)
async def list_posts(...): ...
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph LR
    APP["FastAPI App"] -->|"inspects"| ROUTES["Route definitions<br/>@router.get/post/..."]
    ROUTES --> PYDANTIC["Pydantic schemas<br/>request + response models"]
    ROUTES --> PARAMS["Path / Query / Header<br/>parameters + validators"]
    APP --> OPENAPI["/openapi.json<br/>OpenAPI 3.1 schema"]
    OPENAPI --> SWAGGER["/docs<br/>Swagger UI<br/>(interactive try-it-out)"]
    OPENAPI --> REDOC["/redoc<br/>ReDoc<br/>(read-only reference)"]
    OPENAPI --> CLIENT["Code generators<br/>openapi-generator<br/>fastapi-code-generator"]

    classDef app    fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef source fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef doc    fill:#0a3d0a,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    class APP app
    class ROUTES,PYDANTIC,PARAMS source
    class OPENAPI,SWAGGER,REDOC,CLIENT doc
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
> FastAPI generates `/openapi.json` **automatically** from your code — zero manual YAML/JSON schema writing. It updates live as you change the code.
:::
:::
:::

---

## Testing {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
# tests/test_posts.py
import pytest
from httpx import AsyncClient, ASGITransport
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import create_async_engine, async_sessionmaker
from main import app
from db.session import get_db
from db.base import Base

# ─── Test database setup ───────────────────────────────────
TEST_DATABASE_URL = "sqlite+aiosqlite:///:memory:"

@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
async def test_engine():
    engine = create_async_engine(TEST_DATABASE_URL)
    async with engine.begin() as conn:
        await conn.run_sync(Base.metadata.create_all)
    yield engine
    await engine.dispose()

@pytest.fixture
async def db_session(test_engine):
    session_maker = async_sessionmaker(test_engine)
    async with session_maker() as session:
        yield session
        await session.rollback()

@pytest.fixture
async def client(db_session):
    # Override the real DB dependency with the test DB
    app.dependency_overrides[get_db] = lambda: db_session
    async with AsyncClient(
        transport=ASGITransport(app=app), base_url="http://test"
    ) as ac:
        yield ac
    app.dependency_overrides.clear()

# ─── Tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────
@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_create_post(client, auth_headers):
    response = await client.post(
        "/api/v1/posts/",
        json={"title": "Test Post", "content": "Hello World",
              "category_id": 1},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert response.status_code == 201
    data = response.json()
    assert data["title"] == "Test Post"
    assert "id" in data

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_get_post_not_found(client):
    response = await client.get("/api/v1/posts/99999")
    assert response.status_code == 404
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    SUITE["pytest test suite"] --> FIXTURES["Fixtures<br/>(scope=session/function)"]
    FIXTURES --> ENGINE["Test SQLite<br/>in-memory DB"]
    FIXTURES --> SESSION["db_session<br/>(rolled back after each test)"]
    FIXTURES --> CLIENT["AsyncClient<br/>(ASGITransport)"]
    CLIENT --> OVERRIDE["dependency_overrides<br/>{get_db: test_db}"]
    OVERRIDE --> APP["FastAPI App<br/>(no real server needed)"]
    APP --> ASSERT["Assertions<br/>assert status_code<br/>assert response.json()"]

    classDef root  fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef fix   fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef infra fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef ok    fill:#0a3d0a,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    class SUITE root
    class FIXTURES,ENGINE,SESSION,CLIENT,OVERRIDE fix
    class APP infra
    class ASSERT ok
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
```bash
pip install pytest pytest-asyncio httpx aiosqlite
pytest -v --asyncio-mode=auto
pytest tests/ -k "post" --cov=app --cov-report=term
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Settings & Configuration {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
```python
# core/settings.py
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings, SettingsConfigDict
from functools import lru_cache

class Settings(BaseSettings):
    # App
    app_name:    str = "My FastAPI App"
    debug:       bool = False
    environment: str = "production"

    # Database
    database_url:       str
    database_pool_size: int = 10

    # Security
    secret_key:                  str
    algorithm:                   str = "HS256"
    access_token_expire_minutes: int = 30

    # Redis
    redis_url: str = "redis://localhost:6379/0"

    # External APIs
    sendgrid_api_key: str = ""
    aws_s3_bucket:    str = ""

    model_config = SettingsConfigDict(
        env_file=".env",
        env_file_encoding="utf-8",
        case_sensitive=False,
    )

@lru_cache
def get_settings() -> Settings:
    return Settings()

# Usage anywhere in the app
settings = get_settings()
# or as a FastAPI dependency:
# def route(settings: Settings = Depends(get_settings)):
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
**`.env` file**
```bash
DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://user:pass@db/mydb
SECRET_KEY=super-secret-key-change-me
DEBUG=false
ENVIRONMENT=production
DATABASE_POOL_SIZE=20
ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES=60
REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379/0
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    ENV[".env file<br/>(gitignored)"] --> SETTINGS["pydantic_settings<br/>BaseSettings<br/>(type-validated)"]
    ENV_VAR["Environment Variables<br/>(Docker / K8s secrets)"] --> SETTINGS
    SETTINGS -->|"@lru_cache"| CACHED["Cached singleton<br/>get_settings()"]
    CACHED --> APP["FastAPI App<br/>dependencies / modules"]

    classDef src   fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef core  fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef app   fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    class ENV,ENV_VAR src
    class SETTINGS,CACHED core
    class APP app
```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Deployment Architecture {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark', 'flowchart': {'curve': 'orthogonal'}}}%%
graph TD
    USER["🌐 Users"] -->|HTTPS| CDN["CDN<br/>(CloudFront / Cloudflare)<br/>Static assets"]
    USER -->|HTTPS| LB["Load Balancer<br/>(Nginx / AWS ALB)"]
    LB  -->|HTTP/2| UV1["Uvicorn Worker 1<br/>(ASGI)"]
    LB  -->|HTTP/2| UV2["Uvicorn Worker 2<br/>(ASGI)"]
    LB  -->|HTTP/2| UV3["Uvicorn Worker N<br/>(ASGI)"]
    UV1 -->|async| APP["FastAPI Application"]
    UV2 -->|async| APP
    UV3 -->|async| APP
    APP -->|asyncpg| DB["PostgreSQL<br/>(Primary)"]
    DB  -->|replication| DBREP["PostgreSQL Replica<br/>(Read-only)"]
    APP -->|aioredis| REDIS["Redis<br/>(Cache + Sessions<br/>+ Celery broker)"]
    APP -->|enqueue| CELERY["Celery Workers<br/>(Async Tasks)"]
    CELERY --> REDIS
    APP -->|S3 SDK| S3["Object Storage<br/>(S3 / GCS)"]
    CDN -.-> S3

    classDef user  fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef infra fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef app   fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef data  fill:#16213e,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
    class USER user
    class CDN,LB,UV1,UV2,UV3 infra
    class APP,CELERY app
    class DB,DBREP,REDIS,S3 data
```

---

## Dockerfile & Docker Compose {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
**`Dockerfile`**
```dockerfile
FROM python:3.12-slim

WORKDIR /app

# Install dependencies first (layer caching)
COPY pyproject.toml poetry.lock ./
RUN pip install poetry && \
    poetry config virtualenvs.create false && \
    poetry install --no-dev

COPY . .

# Run Alembic migrations then start Uvicorn
CMD ["sh", "-c", \
     "alembic upgrade head && \
      uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 \
      --workers 4 --loop uvloop --http httptools"]
```

::: {.fragment}
**`docker-compose.yml`**
```yaml
services:
  api:
    build: .
    ports: ["8000:8000"]
    env_file: .env
    depends_on: [db, redis]

  db:
    image: postgres:16
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB:       mydb
      POSTGRES_USER:     user
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: pass
    volumes: [pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data]

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine

  celery_worker:
    build: .
    command: celery -A tasks worker --loglevel=info
    env_file: .env
    depends_on: [db, redis]

volumes:
  pgdata:
```
:::
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
::: {.fragment}
```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph TD
    COMPOSE["docker-compose up"] --> API["api container<br/>uvicorn main:app<br/>:8000"]
    COMPOSE --> DB["db container<br/>PostgreSQL :5432"]
    COMPOSE --> REDIS["redis container<br/>Redis :6379"]
    COMPOSE --> WORKER["celery_worker<br/>container"]

    API --> DB
    API --> REDIS
    WORKER --> DB
    WORKER --> REDIS

    classDef compose fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef svc     fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    class COMPOSE compose
    class API,DB,REDIS,WORKER svc
```
:::

::: {.fragment}
> For production, add **Gunicorn as process manager** wrapping Uvicorn workers:  
> ```bash
> gunicorn main:app -w 4 -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker
> ```
:::
:::
:::

---

## Production Checklist {.scrollable background-color="#16213e"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Security**

- [ ] `SECRET_KEY` from env variable (never hardcode)
- [ ] `debug=False` in production
- [ ] HTTPS enforced at load balancer
- [ ] CORS restricted to known origins
- [ ] Rate limiting (slowapi / nginx)
- [ ] JWT expiry set short (30–60 min)
- [ ] Refresh token rotation implemented
- [ ] Pydantic models validate all input

**Database**

- [ ] PostgreSQL with asyncpg (not SQLite)
- [ ] Connection pool sized correctly
- [ ] Alembic migrations in CI/CD pipeline
- [ ] DB credentials from secrets manager
- [ ] Regular automated backups
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Performance**

- [ ] `uvloop` event loop (`--loop uvloop`)
- [ ] `httptools` HTTP parser
- [ ] `select_related` / joined loads in queries
- [ ] Redis cache for expensive queries
- [ ] Response compression (GZipMiddleware)
- [ ] Static files via CDN, not FastAPI

**Operations**

- [ ] Gunicorn + UvicornWorker process manager
- [ ] Structured JSON logging (`loguru` / `structlog`)
- [ ] Sentry for error tracking
- [ ] Health check endpoint `/health`
- [ ] Prometheus metrics (`prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator`)
- [ ] OpenAPI docs disabled in prod (`docs_url=None`)
- [ ] Celery for async tasks > 1 sec
- [ ] Graceful shutdown via SIGTERM handler
:::
:::

---

## FastAPI Ecosystem {.scrollable background-color="#0f3460"}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
mindmap
  root((FastAPI<br/>Ecosystem))
    Data Validation
      pydantic v2
      pydantic-settings
      pydantic-extra-types
    Database
      SQLAlchemy 2.0
      SQLModel
      Tortoise ORM
      Beanie (MongoDB)
      asyncpg / aiosqlite
    Authentication
      python-jose (JWT)
      passlib (bcrypt)
      FastAPI-Users
      Authlib (OAuth2)
    Testing
      pytest-asyncio
      httpx
      factory-boy
      Faker
    Tasks & Scheduling
      Celery + Redis
      ARQ (async)
      APScheduler
    Monitoring
      prometheus-fastapi
      OpenTelemetry
      Sentry SDK
      loguru
    Deployment
      uvicorn
      gunicorn
      uvloop
      httptools
    API Extras
      slowapi (rate limit)
      fastapi-cache2
      fastapi-pagination
      fastapi-mail
```

---

## Summary {.scrollable background-color="#1a1a2e"}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%
graph LR
    subgraph REQUEST["Request Phase ▶"]
        UV["Uvicorn<br/>(ASGI)"] --> MW["Middleware<br/>(CORS, Auth, Timing)"]
        MW --> ROUTER["APIRouter<br/>(path match)"]
        ROUTER --> DI["Dependency<br/>Injection"]
    end

    subgraph PROCESS["Processing"]
        DI --> PYDANTIC["Pydantic<br/>(validate input)"]
        PYDANTIC --> VIEW["Path Operation<br/>Function"]
        VIEW -->|"await"| DB[(Database)]
        DB --> VIEW
        VIEW -->|"await"| CACHE["Redis<br/>Cache"]
        CACHE --> VIEW
    end

    subgraph RESPONSE["◀ Response Phase"]
        VIEW --> SERIAL["Pydantic<br/>(serialize output)"]
        SERIAL --> STATUS["Status Code<br/>+ Headers"]
        STATUS --> CLIENT["JSON Response<br/>to Client"]
    end

    subgraph ASYNC["Fire & Forget"]
        VIEW --> BG["BackgroundTasks<br/>/ Celery"]
    end

    classDef req  fill:#e94560,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef proc fill:#0f3460,stroke:#aaa,color:#fff
    classDef resp fill:#533483,stroke:none,color:#fff
    classDef bg   fill:#16213e,stroke:#2ecc71,color:#fff
```

::: {.fragment}
**Key Takeaways:**

- **Async-first** — true non-blocking I/O via ASGI/uvicorn; handle thousands of concurrent connections
- **Type hints everywhere** — Pydantic v2 validates all input/output automatically; 422 errors are free
- **Auto-generated OpenAPI docs** — `/docs` and `/redoc` update live with your code; no manual YAML
- **Dependency Injection** — clean, testable, composable; swap deps for testing with `dependency_overrides`
- **Standards-based** — OAuth2, JWT, JSON Schema, OpenAPI 3 — all implemented correctly out of the box
:::
